title: How to Understand Emerging Risks
content: Understanding and anticipating risks is crucial to protecting society from the unpredictable. But the nature of risk is evolving at an unprecedented pace, and the processes and assumptions that guide insurers and their clients in managing uncertainty must be adjusted accordingly.

creator: Françoise Gilles

title: The Iran War’s Winners and Losers
content: The current ceasefire allows us to make a preliminary assessment of the war and its effects. China and Russia were clear winners, while the Gulf Arab states suffered a reputational hit, and the combatants all had decidedly mixed outcomes, although the United States and Israel gained the least.

creator: Richard Haass

title: No Exit from Stalin
content: While Russia has not reverted to full-bore Stalinism, there is no doubt that President Vladimir Putin has made tremendous strides toward totalitarianism. That is why people have sought lessons in history—in particular, in the story of how calls for change emerged at the commanding heights of the USSR.
snippet: While Russia has not reverted to full-bore Stalinism, there is no doubt that President Vladimir Putin has made tremendous strides toward totalitarianism. That is why people have sought lessons in hist
creator: Nina L. Khrushcheva
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s War Is Adding to Africa’s Debt Burden
content: The US-Israeli war against Iran has shown, once again, that when American policy decisions push Treasury yields higher, African sovereigns pay the price. African governments must ensure that this is the last geopolitical crisis that drives up their borrowing costs through no fault of their own.
snippet: The US-Israeli war against Iran has shown, once again, that when American policy decisions push Treasury yields higher, African sovereigns pay the price. African governments must ensure that this is t
creator: Vera Songwe
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Decline and Fall of the Dollar Empire
content: The United States does not have an emperor like ancient Rome's Nero, but its politics are increasingly subject to rule by one man who threatens its democratic traditions. And just as Nero undermined the Roman denarius—arguably the world's first international currency—America's ruler poses a growing threat to his country's currency.
snippet: The United States does not have an emperor like ancient Rome's Nero, but its politics are increasingly subject to rule by one man who threatens its democratic traditions. And just as Nero undermined t
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s Next Coup Attempt
content: While turning a foreign war into a domestic dictatorship is complicated and difficult, Donald Trump could try to do so in one of five ways. But even the most likely scenario—using an act of terror as a pretext to delay or discredit the midterms—will not work if Americans are vigilant and refuse to obey in advance.

creator: Timothy Snyder
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran’s Strategic Victory
content: The US-Israeli war will be remembered as yet another episode of powerful countries falling into the trap of asymmetric warfare, with the ceasefire ratifying what any competent military planner should have anticipated. But while the US might be able to absorb the shock of another defeat, Israel is no superpower.

creator: Shlomo Ben-Ami
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Problem of Assessing Democratic Decline
content: Democracy is in retreat across much of the world, erasing nearly five decades of hard-won gains. Understanding this trend and how to reverse it requires better ways to evaluate democratic governance and an honest reckoning with how major powers undermine rights and norms beyond their own borders.

creator: Kaushik Basu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Fall of Gazprom
content: Eastern European energy-sector dynamics have become an accurate signal of broader macroeconomic trends. Through the Polish, Russian, and Hungarian energy conglomerates, one can glimpse three political-economic models at work, only one of which holds any promise for sustained, healthy development.
snippet: Eastern European energy-sector dynamics have become an accurate signal of broader macroeconomic trends. Through the Polish, Russian, and Hungarian energy conglomerates, one can glimpse three political
creator: Sławomir Sierakowski
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The IMF’s Spring Meetings Must Deliver Three Reforms
content: Kenya’s recent decision to forgo funding from the International Monetary Fund illustrates the asymmetry at the heart of the multilateral financial architecture. As policymakers gather for the Spring Meetings, they have an opportunity to address these structural imbalances, starting with an overhaul of the IMF’s quota system.

creator: Attiya Waris
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China Is Having a Good War—So Far
content: So far, China is weathering the US/Israeli war with Iran better than many of its neighbors, and it is treading cautiously as it seeks opportunities to profit from the fallout. While other major powers commit spectacular strategic blunders, Chinese policymakers are watching and learning.

creator: Ian Bremmer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War Is a Boon for the BRICS
content: More than a month into the US-Israeli war against Iran, the broader strategic implications are coming into sharper focus. While the two-week ceasefire has provided Donald Trump with a possible off-ramp, China, India, and other powers suddenly find themselves in a much stronger geopolitical position.

creator: Jim O'Neill
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can Hungary Reverse Course?
content: As Hungarians head to the polls, the rest of the world will be watching closely to see if Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's carefully crafted system of free but unfair elections is still functioning as intended. With his challenger commanding a double-digit lead in opinion polls, three scenarios remain possible.
snippet: As Hungarians head to the polls, the rest of the world will be watching closely to see if Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's carefully crafted system of free but unfair elections is still functioning as in
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Coming Inflation-Deflation Whipsaw
content: Other than the pandemic-era supply-chain disruptions, business leaders and investors have not had occasion to think much about either inflation or deflation. But that needs to change, because we are now facing both an immediate threat of higher prices and a longer-term prospect of rapidly falling labor, goods, and services costs.
snippet: Other than the pandemic-era supply-chain disruptions, business leaders and investors have not had occasion to think much about either inflation or deflation. But that needs to change, because we are n
creator: Dambisa Moyo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Road to De-Escalation With Iran
content: Around the world, cash-strapped governments and people already struggling with poverty and precarity are asking with increasing urgency: Is there an off-ramp for the latest conflict in the Middle East? There is, but it requires taking economic incentives seriously.
snippet: Around the world, cash-strapped governments and people already struggling with poverty and precarity are asking with increasing urgency: Is there an off-ramp for the latest conflict in the Middle East
creator: Simon Johnson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Israel’s Buffer-Zone Fallacy
content: In an era when ballistic missiles, drones, and other projectiles can travel long distances to hit targets with growing accuracy, the idea of a protective buffer zone is not only faulty; it is complete nonsense. Yet this is precisely the rationale that Israel has offered for its occupation of southern Lebanon.

creator: Daoud Kuttab
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Women Succeed in Male-Dominated Fields
content: Discussions about gender and work often morph into cultural clashes, driven by fear, ideology, or frustration. But recent empirical research suggests that having female peers and professors can help women succeed in the workplace without hurting men.
snippet: Discussions about gender and work often morph into cultural clashes, driven by fear, ideology, or frustration. But recent empirical research suggests that having female peers and professors can help w
creator: Bruna Borges
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real AI Race
content: While the invention of general-purpose technologies creates new opportunities, it is their diffusion across industries and economies that drives economic transformation. Early evidence suggests that AI is likely to follow a similar trajectory.
snippet: While the invention of general-purpose technologies creates new opportunities, it is their diffusion across industries and economies that drives economic transformation. Early evidence suggests that A
creator: Lee Jong-Wha
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Is Economic Forecasting Still Possible?
content: Much of the forecasting profession—and the economic theory that underpins it—still defaults to single baselines that treat the future as a probabilistic replica of the past. But when the structure of the economy changes in unforeseeable ways, as it is now, forecasters must acknowledge that many futures are possible.

creator: Morten Nyboe Tabor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can India Become the World’s Innovation Capital?
content: As supply chains fragment, AI rewrites the rules of competition, and geopolitical volatility intensifies, companies are searching for stable, scalable, innovation‑rich environments. India can provide what they need, and with the right enabling policies, it can transform its advantages into a lasting global lead.

creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Type of Democratic Transition
content: When trying to rebuild after illiberal rule, today’s pro-democracy governments discover booby traps laid by their predecessors and confront all kinds of veto players. But Poland’s experience has been instructive, highlighting the need for bold action and the right support from external actors.
snippet: When trying to rebuild after illiberal rule, today’s pro-democracy governments discover booby traps laid by their predecessors and confront all kinds of veto players. But Poland’s experience has been
creator: Jan-Werner Mueller

title: How Bad Will the Economy Be?
content: Economic performance in the United States has proved unexpectedly resilient in recent years, withstanding even Donald Trump’s return to the White House. But, from a failed war of choice against Iran to reckless deregulation, fundamental risks are piling up, and uncertainty has become so extreme that economic forecasters cannot even assign probabilities to them. While that rules out confident prediction, the global connectedness of these risks means that what comes next is certain to reverberate worldwide.

title: The Real Question About the AI Future
content: Whereas US power in the 20th century rested heavily on manufacturing scale, military reach, and dollar strength, in the 21st century it may rest increasingly on ownership of indispensable AI infrastructure. The challenge for the world will be how to pay for access to it.
